subheading: Run Lightricks' open LTX video model in ComfyUI: text-to-video, image-to-video, and synchronized audio, on Comfy Cloud or your own GPU.
extendedDescription:
If you are weighing LTX comfyui workflows, the thing that sets LTX apart is that it is a genuinely open video model that also generates sound. LTX is Lightricks' diffusion-transformer family, and the current LTX-2 line was released with open weights, so you can run it on your own hardware instead of renting it by the second. The workflows on this page are the ready-made graphs for that: load an LTX model, give it a prompt or an image, and generate a clip with audio already aligned to the picture.

LTX covers the usual modes and a few extras. Text-to-video turns a prompt into motion, image-to-video animates a still, and keyframe conditioning lets you set frames the clip should pass through, including a last-frame interpolation in the newer release. LTX-2 produces synchronized audio and video together at up to 4K and up to 50 frames per second, with clips up to around twenty seconds. The LTX-2.3 point release sharpens detail with a redesigned VAE, cleans up the audio, and adds native portrait video at 1080 by 1920 for vertical formats.

VRAM is the main thing to plan around. The full 22B LTX-2.3 model documents a 32GB requirement, but FP8 and community GGUF builds bring that down so capable consumer cards can run it, and a distilled variant finishes in about eight steps for faster iteration. Because the graph is open, you control the steps, the resolution, and the conditioning, and you can re-run until the motion reads right.

Everything here is open and editable. Run an LTX workflow on Comfy Cloud with nothing to install when you do not have the VRAM, or download it and run it locally. LTX-2 ships under the LTX-2 Community License, which permits research and commercial use by companies under 10 million dollars in revenue at no cost, and there is no watermark on the output.

modelSpec.summary: LTX is Lightricks' open diffusion-transformer video family. The 22B LTX-2 line generates synchronized audio and video up to 4K, with LTX-2.3 adding sharper detail and native portrait output.

highlights.title: Open weights
highlights.description: Open weights from Lightricks under the LTX-2 Community License (no cost under $10M revenue).

highlights.title: Audio and video
highlights.description: Synchronized audio and video in a single model, up to 4K and up to 50fps.

highlights.title: Video modes
highlights.description: Text-to-video, image-to-video, and keyframe conditioning with last-frame interpolation.

highlights.title: 2.3 upgrades
highlights.description: LTX-2.3 adds a redesigned VAE for detail, cleaner audio, and native 1080x1920 portrait.

highlights.title: Consumer VRAM
highlights.description: 22B model documents 32GB VRAM; FP8 and GGUF builds run on capable consumer cards.

highlights.title: Eight-step variant
highlights.description: A distilled variant finishes in about eight steps; runnable on Comfy Cloud with no install.

howToUse: Pick an LTX workflow that matches your goal: text-to-video, image-to-video, or keyframe.
Open it on Comfy Cloud with no install, or load it in your local ComfyUI.
Choose a build for your VRAM, such as an FP8 or distilled LTX-2.3 variant.
Add your prompt, or upload the still image or keyframes the workflow expects.
Set the steps, resolution, and frame rate, then run and review the motion and audio.
Re-run with adjusted settings, then download the clip with no watermark.

whyComfy.title: Audio and video from one open model
whyComfy.description: LTX-2 generates synchronized sound and picture together, up to 4K and 50fps, instead of stitching audio on in a separate pass.

whyComfy.title: Pick your variant and settings
whyComfy.description: Switch between full 22B LTX-2.3, FP8, GGUF, and the eight-step distilled build, then tune steps, resolution, frame rate, and keyframes.

whyComfy.title: Open weights you can run anywhere
whyComfy.description: Run LTX locally on your own GPU under the LTX-2 Community License, or on Comfy Cloud with no install when you are short on VRAM.

whyComfy.title: Native portrait and no watermark
whyComfy.description: LTX-2.3 outputs native 1080x1920 vertical video and a redesigned VAE for sharper detail, and the clips you generate carry no watermark.

faqItems.question: What are LTX ComfyUI workflows?
faqItems.answer: They are ready-made ComfyUI graphs that run LTX, the open video model from Lightricks. Each loads an LTX model and wires up the inputs for a task like ltx text to video or ltx image to video, so you give a prompt or image and get a clip with aligned audio without building the graph yourself.

faqItems.question: Is LTX open source?
faqItems.answer: LTX ships with open weights. The LTX-2 line uses the LTX-2 Community License, which permits research and commercial use by companies under 10 million dollars in revenue at no cost, while the older LTX-Video 0.9.x line is Apache 2.0. The workflows run it locally or on Comfy Cloud with no watermark.

faqItems.question: Does LTX generate audio with the video?
faqItems.answer: Yes. The LTX-2 line generates synchronized audio and video together in a single model, so sound is aligned to the picture rather than added in a separate step. LTX-2.3 further cleans up that audio by filtering silence gaps and noise.

faqItems.question: How much VRAM does LTX need?
faqItems.answer: The full 22B LTX-2.3 model documents a 32GB VRAM requirement, but FP8 and community GGUF builds bring that down so capable consumer cards can run it. Comfy Cloud removes the requirement entirely by running the workflow in the browser.

faqItems.question: What does LTX-2.3 add over LTX-2?
faqItems.answer: LTX-2.3 is a point release that sharpens fine detail with a redesigned VAE, improves prompt adherence, and cleans up the generated audio. It also adds native portrait video at 1080 by 1920 and extra frame-rate options for vertical formats.

faqItems.question: Can I run LTX without a powerful GPU?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Run any LTX workflow on Comfy Cloud in the browser with nothing to install when your card is not enough. If you do have a capable GPU, download the workflow and run an FP8 or distilled build locally for faster iteration.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything to use these workflows?
faqItems.answer: Not on Comfy Cloud, where the workflow opens in your browser with the LTX weights already in place and nothing to set up. To run locally instead, install ComfyUI on your own machine and download the LTX model build that fits your VRAM.

faqItems.question: Does LTX run locally or only on Comfy Cloud?
faqItems.answer: Both. LTX-2 ships with open weights, so you can download it and run an FP8 or distilled build on your own GPU. Comfy Cloud is the paid option for the same graphs when your hardware is short on the VRAM the 22B model wants.
